title: Intensely Flavorful Irish Lamb Stew
description: A hearty and aromatic stew featuring lamb, lemon, and puff pastry, perfect for special occasions. This dish is sure to please a crowd with its rich and dense flavor profile.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large Dutch oven, brown the lamb cubes over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion, carrots, and potatoes to the pot and cook until the vegetables are tender.
Add the garlic, thyme, and rosemary to the pot and cook for 1 minute.
Add the lemon juice and chicken broth to the pot and bring to a boil.
Cover the pot with puff pastry and cut a few slits in the top to allow steam to escape.
Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and bake for 2 1/2 hours.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes before serving.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
